Details: One study reported that chlortetracycline was removed using electrochemical oxidation [1528]. Three electrolytes were tested: sodium sulfate, sodium nitrate and sodium chloride. Chloride was the best electrolyte for chlortetracycline removal, and the study suggested this was due to indirect oxidation effect of chlorine/hypochlorite. Chlortetracycline removal efficiency increased with increasing electrolysis time. The most removal was observed after 180 minutes of electrolysis using sodium chloride.
